name: SSRF hidden inside an 'extracted helper' refactor — analyzer must connect changes across 3 files
challenge: The change spans (1) preview.ts where the WHITELIST is removed, (2) utils/url.ts where a NEW helper is created that performs only a syntactic URL check (no allowlist), and (3) test/preview.test.ts which gives the false reassurance that 'rejects unknown targets' is still tested. Analyzer must understand that resolveTarget() does NOT replicate the allowlist behaviour, despite reading like it does. A purely line-by-line analysis of preview.ts alone might mark this as LIKELY_TP because the dangerous sink is now reachable through any HTTPS URL.
notes: This is the canonical 'refactor hides a regression' pattern. Production diff reviews often look like this — a refactor that mechanically preserves the API shape but loosens semantics. The test file change is a red herring: a passing 'rejects unknown' test no longer means what its name suggests, because 'unknown' now matches `^https?://` not `WHITELIST[key]`.
